title: Enhancing climate analysis: new insights through data
summary: The ECB has published a blog post detailing recent enhancements to the ESCB's statistical climate indicators, including new breakdowns of sustainable debt securities by maturity, interest rate type, and currency, as well as methodology improvements for assessing how inflation affects banks' carbon intensity metrics. The analysis finds that while most euro area sustainable bonds are denominated in euro, sustainability-linked and social bonds have significantly higher proportions of non-euro issuances (20.5% and 23.7% respectively) compared to green bonds (7.3%).
key_points: New sustainable debt securities data now includes breakdowns by maturity, interest rate type, and currency for green, social, sustainability-linked, and sustainability bonds
Green and sustainability bonds in the euro area are predominantly euro-denominated (92.7% and 87.2% respectively), mirroring broader euro area debt securities trends (90% euro-denominated)
Sustainability-linked and social bonds have higher non-euro issuance shares at 20.5% and 23.7% respectively
Non-euro area investors hold 34% of social bonds, 25% of sustainability-linked and sustainability bonds, and only 18% of green bonds issued by euro area residents (June 2025 data)
Analysis of carbon intensity drivers shows that inflation contributed to portfolio carbon intensity decline in 2022 and 2023, with the largest impact in 2022 coinciding with elevated HICP inflation
Ignoring inflation in carbon intensity calculations leads to underestimating actual carbon intensity, as nominal revenue increases can overstate real decarbonization progress
why_it_matters: The why_it_matters correctly captures the analytical value but could note this represents ECB staff research methodology rather than official policy findings.
macro_context: The blog post provides methodological enhancements for climate data analysis that could inform macroprudential assessment of climate-related financial risks. The finding that inflation can materially affect carbon intensity metrics is relevant for evaluating whether bank portfolio greening is genuine or partly a nominal effect.
market_structure_context: The source provides data on the currency composition and investor base of different sustainable debt instrument types in the euro area, which offers insights into the structure of euro area sustainable capital markets.
important_numbers: 34% of social bonds held by non-euro area investors
25% of sustainability-linked and sustainability bonds held by non-euro area investors
18% of green bonds held by non-euro area investors
